name: Discone Antenna
description: A broadband omnidirectional antenna consisting of a disc and cone configuration that provides wide frequency coverage with consistent radiation patterns. The disc acts as a ground plane while the cone serves as the radiating element, creating a vertically polarized antenna with excellent broadband characteristics. Commonly used in applications requiring wide frequency coverage from VHF through microwave frequencies.

disc_diameter.name: Disc Diameter
disc_diameter.description: Diameter of the disc element that acts as ground plane

cone_diameter.name: Cone Base Diameter
cone_diameter.description: Diameter of the cone base at the feed point

cone_height.name: Cone Height
cone_height.description: Height of the cone element from base to apex

cone_angle.name: Cone Half Angle
cone_angle.description: Half angle of the cone measured from vertical axis

gap_height.name: Gap Height
gap_height.description: Vertical separation between disc and cone base

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining optimal disc and cone dimensions based on desired frequency range, calculating cone angle for impedance matching, and optimizing gap spacing for broadband performance.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency Range
steps.description: Define the lowest and highest operating frequencies to establish wavelength constraints
steps.formula: lambda_max = c / f_min, lambda_min = c / f_max

steps.title: Calculate Disc Diameter
steps.description: Size the disc to provide adequate ground plane at the lowest frequency

steps.title: Determine Cone Dimensions
steps.description: Calculate cone base diameter and height for optimal impedance matching

steps.title: Calculate Cone Half Angle
steps.description: Determine the cone angle that provides 50-ohm impedance match

steps.title: Set Gap Height
steps.description: Establish vertical gap between disc and cone for proper feed structure

steps.title: Optimize for VSWR
steps.description: Fine-tune dimensions to achieve VSWR < 2:1 across the band

equations.resonantFrequency: f_res = c / (4 * H_cone) for fundamental mode
equations.inputImpedance: Z_in ≈ 377 * sin(alpha) for small cone angles
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta) = sin(theta) * cos(alpha) for vertical plane

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with tapered cone impedance transformation from disc ground plane to free space
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply method of moments with triangular surface patches on cone and disc, accounting for edge diffraction effects
keyAssumptions: Perfect conductor assumption for disc and cone
Infinite ground plane approximation for disc
Linear taper impedance transformation along cone
Negligible mutual coupling between disc and cone edges

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 9
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 8
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 15
